target.name: Majestic Labs
target.founder: Ofer Shacham, Sha Rabii, Masumi Reynders

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Majestic Labs is a Series A-stage semiconductor design company founded by Ofer Shacham, Sha Rabii, and Masumi Reynders, with $100M+ in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 4 private peers in the Chips & Semiconductors sector at the same stage—Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M)—alongside 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $100M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a standard 2.0x Series A step-up multiplier, implies a $200M valuation. Peer-median methodology yields $1.5B, calculated from the median of the 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring using the semiconductor sector's 25.3x price-to-sales multiple cannot be applied due to unavailable revenue data. Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a low-to-high range of $170M to $1.7B, with a median of $850M. Key uncertainties center on the wide dispersion within the peer set—driven by divergent technology maturity and market positioning—and the absence of the target's revenue profile, which would enable public-market cross-validation.
